title: Sean Penn Ties Oscar Record for Male Acting Wins
text: Sean Penn won the Academy Award for Best Supporting Actor at the 2026 Oscars for his role in One Battle After Another, achieving a historic milestone. With this victory, Penn joins an exclusive group of male actors with three acting Oscars each: Jack Nicholson, Walter Brennan, and Daniel Day-Lewis. Penn portrayed Col. Steven J. Lockjaw in the film, described as a vivid avatar for contemporary American authoritarianism. This marks his first supporting actor win, though he has previously earned two lead acting Oscars for Mystic River and Milk. The win came after Penn secured victories at both the BAFTA and SAG-AFTRA awards, which typically align closely with Academy voters. However, the category proved competitive, with Penn having lost the Critics Choice Award to Jacob Elordi and the Golden Globe to Stellan Skarsgard. Delroy Lindo and Penn's co-star Benicio Del Toro also received nominations. Penn notably did not attend the ceremony, with Kieran Culkin accepting the award on his behalf. This absence continued a pattern of limited campaign visibility that included missing the actor awards and BAFTA ceremony.
